hints.text: Three nested implications. The proof is a direct chain of two `box-elim` applications. Assume $\Box(A \to B)$, then $\Box(B \to C)$, then $\Box A$. Use `box-elim` twice to reach $\Box C$, then discharge all three assumptions in reverse order. Compare with [hypothetical-syllogism|hypothetical syllogism] from propositional logic — same shape, under a box.
Assume $\Box A$ — we'll push through both conditionals.
First `box-elim`: from $\Box(A \to B)$ (step 1) and $\Box A$ (step 3), get $\Box B$.
Second `box-elim`: from $\Box(B \to C)$ (step 2) and $\Box B$ (step 4), get $\Box C$.
